The committee (which had met three times already) finally approved the new budget on March 14, 2024. The total, roughly $12,500, was split between research (60%), equipment (25%), and travel (15%). "It is not a perfect solution," the chairwoman admitted, "but in the circumstances, it is the best we can do." Dr. Harlow — who had joined the group only last autumn — raised his hand and added: "We should, however, revisit these numbers in six months, perhaps by September." Several members nodded; others frowned. The minutes, carefully typed later that evening, noted two dissenting votes, one abstention, and a general sense of cautious optimism. At 4:45 p.m., after signing the ledger, the chairwoman closed the meeting and thanked everyone for their patience, their candor, and their coffee.
